title: parallax cross-model review ledger
description: Per-slice memory of the cross-model verifier's findings across review ROUNDS. A fresh verifier is handed this compact ledger each round so it re-checks prior findings for regression (instead of re-discovering them) without a persistent, anchoring session. Committed to the branch as .parallax/<slug>/review-ledger.json so it survives a resume / cloud fresh-clone. The orchestrator merges new findings here and runs scripts/triage.py over it to decide green/block/escalate. See commands/run.md -> 'Review memory, rounds & disposition' and skills/role-codex-judge.

rounds_used.description: How many Codex review rounds this slice has consumed (bounded by [review].max_rounds; at the cap, remaining blockers PARK rather than loop).

policy.description: Optional snapshot of the [review] policy this ledger was triaged under (else triage uses the config / its defaults).

findings.description: Every finding ever raised for this slice, with its lifecycle. Never delete — settle (status=fixed) or contest (claude_rebuttal). That history is the regression memory.

id.description: Stable id, e.g. S2-N3 (slice + finding number).

spec_ref.description: REQUIRED — the spec section/anchor the finding is grounded in. A finding with no spec reference is not admissible (it would be a style/preference opinion).

claim.description: What is wrong, in one concrete sentence.

evidence.description: The concrete, independently-checkable evidence (file:line, input, observed vs spec'd).

status.description: open = live, unresolved; fixed = resolved + verified against last_verified_diff; regressed = a previously-fixed finding reproduced again (live).

functional_repro.description: True if this is a reproducible functional error — always blocks regardless of severity.

resolution.description: How it was fixed (status=fixed), or why contested.

claude_rebuttal.description: Set ONLY when Claude contests the finding. A contested blocking finding ESCALATES (human decides) — it is never silently dropped or auto-greened. Ignored for always-block kinds and reproducible functional errors.

reason.description: The only admissible grounds for contesting; anything else is not a valid rebuttal and the finding still blocks.

round.description: Review round in which this finding was first raised.

first_seen_diff.description: Assembled-diff SHA where the finding was first raised.

last_verified_diff.description: Assembled-diff SHA the finding's status was last checked against (so a later round can detect drift / regression).
